title: sorbus aucuparia var. lanuginosa
intro: Sorbus aucuparia var. lanuginosa, also known as the downy whitebeam, is a variation of the mountain ash tree, characterized by its distinctive downy leaves and white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sorbus aucuparia var. lanuginosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, and in some cases, the bark and leaves are dried and used in folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
